name: Beholder Tyrant

headline.name: Eye Tyrant — the Eye Barrage
headline.summary: Each eye-stalk fires a different curse-ray. In the throne fight a tier-gated barrage rotates Petrify, Drain (damage + self-heal), Hex (amplifies your blows) and, at the final act, a Disintegrate death-ray. More eyes open each act.
headline.brief: Eye-stalks fire rotating curse-rays — Petrify, Drain, Hex, then a final Disintegrate death-ray. More eyes open each act.

mechanics.text: Tyrant's Gaze (day active) — arm, click a room, and lock down everyone inside: Silence (T1) → +Slow (T2) → +Petrify (T3) → +Disintegrate damage (T4). Uses scale with boss level; room-wide, so it scales with the crowd.
mechanics.brief: Lock down a room: silence inside, then add slow, petrify, and disintegrate damage as it ranks up.

mechanics.text: Anti-Magic Aura — each day, marks rooms that silence all class abilities inside (the gaze's standing T1 baseline).

flavorText: All seeing. All silencing. Whatever the eye lingers on, the world forgets how to move.

name: Demon Lord

headline.name: The Brimstone Pact
headline.summary: Death is fuel. Sacrifices and every adventurer kill bank INFERNAL POWER (which also regenerates the Demon); the Infernal Pact spends it to rain hellfire on a room — the bigger the reserve, the bigger the cataclysm.
headline.brief: Kills and sacrifices bank Infernal Power; spend it to rain hellfire — the bigger the reserve, the bigger the blast.

mechanics.text: Infernal Pact (day active) — arm, click a room: burn an imp + spend banked Brimstone to hellfire it (bigger reserve = bigger blast). Higher acts add burning ground, heal-block, and a %HP execute that refunds Brimstone.
mechanics.brief: Burn an imp and spend banked Brimstone to hellfire a room; late acts add burning ground and executes.

mechanics.text: Volatile Legion (Act II+) — Hellgate imps EXPLODE in hellfire when a hero kills them. Soul Harvest (Act III+) doubles the Brimstone every death banks. Infernal Ascendance (Act IV) — while the reserve is near full, your whole legion's attacks sear.
Hellgate — a portal in the boss room spawns free Imps every dawn (Pact fuel + Volatile bombs); they roam the dungeon and don't count toward your minion cap.

flavorText: Every contract is signed in someone else's blood. The Pact decides whose.

name: Predator Myconid

headline.name: The Bloom
headline.summary: Colonize the dungeon. Bloomed rooms gas heroes with spores (and, as the Bloom matures, block their healing and slow them) while your minions inside gain regen. Biomass — banked from every death — spreads the colony and feeds the throne.
headline.brief: Colonize the dungeon — bloomed rooms gas heroes with spores while your minions inside regenerate. Biomass spreads the colony.

mechanics.text: Seed the Bloom (day active) — arm, click a room, and instantly colonize it (with a spore-burst as the Bloom matures). The colony also creeps into adjacent rooms on its own at higher acts.
mechanics.brief: Instantly colonize a room with a spore-burst; at higher acts the bloom creeps into neighboring rooms on its own.

mechanics.text: Corpse Bloom — dead adventurers rot into fungal corpses that poison on contact AND auto-bloom their room, feeding the colony.

flavorText: Patient soil keeps every footstep. Eventually it grows tired of them.

name: Dark Wraith

headline.name: The Dread Harvest
headline.summary: Banks DREAD from every fright; it amplifies all fear and drops the break thresholds, so the party panics, knifes itself, and drops dead faster as terror pools.
headline.brief: Every fright banks Dread, amplifying all fear and lowering the break thresholds — the party panics and dies faster.

mechanics.text: Fear → breaks — enough fear makes a hero flee (dropping gold + spreading panic), knife an ally, or die of fright. Every break feeds DREAD.
mechanics.brief: Enough fear makes a hero flee, knife an ally, or die of fright — and every break feeds Dread.

mechanics.text: Night Terror (day) — flood a room with dread: a fear spike, a lingering haunted zone, and at high acts an instant break / mass heart-stop.
Haunting — dead adventurers leave free wall-phasing Ghosts that haunt their room and build the dungeon's DREAD.

flavorText: The bravest die first. The cowards live just long enough to be useful.

name: Gnoll Alpha

headline.name: The Blood Hunt
headline.summary: Carnage banks FEROCITY, whipping the free Hunter's Pack into a faster, fiercer frenzy and fuelling coordinated hunts the Alpha leads in person.
headline.brief: Carnage banks Ferocity, whipping the free Hunter's Pack into a fiercer frenzy and fueling hunts the Alpha leads.

mechanics.text: FEROCITY — every kill + the pack's damage banks FEROCITY; high FEROCITY frenzies the pack (more attack + move speed).
mechanics.brief: Every kill banks Ferocity; high Ferocity frenzies the pack with more attack and move speed.

mechanics.text: Sound the Hunt (day) — the pack converges on a room and savages everyone (%maxHP rend); the Alpha leaps in and, late, the hunt pursues survivors room to room.
Hunter's Pack + Bloodlust — free gnolls respawn each dawn (no cap) and stack ATK per kill for the day.

flavorText: First blood feeds the second. By dusk the teeth are sharper than the gates.

name: Earth Golem

headline.name: The Living Fortress
headline.summary: The dungeon IS its body. Every room you hold grows its HP and DEF and makes its quakes hit harder. The bigger your fortress, the more unkillable the Golem.
headline.brief: The dungeon is its body — every room you hold raises its HP and Defense and makes its quakes hit harder.

mechanics.text: Seismic Slam (day active) — arm, click a room: damage scales with how many rooms you've built. Higher acts add a lingering Fissure (DoT + slow), Collapse that buries heroes, and a Cataclysm that shakes adjacent rooms too.
mechanics.brief: Quake a room for damage that scales with your room count; late acts add fissures, collapses, and cataclysms.

mechanics.text: Aftershock (Act II+) — periodic tremors chip the most-occupied room; Tremor Network (Act III+) quakes every occupied room; Tectonic Upheaval (Act IV) also briefly roots. Constant, dungeon-wide seismic pressure.
Living Architecture — every room you hold adds HP and DEF to the boss; the fortress grows as you build and shrinks if rooms are torn down.

flavorText: Stone keeps every promise. Flesh keeps trying to.

name: Elder Lich

headline.name: The Withering
headline.summary: In the throne fight it spends banked Soul Essence to blast, drain and wither the party — escalating each act — and self-heals while Essence is held. Every death in your halls banks more.
headline.brief: Spend banked Soul Essence to blast, drain, and wither the party, self-healing while it is held. Every death banks more.

mechanics.text: Channel Souls — during the day, spend essence on a target room: blast, drain, wither, then (late acts) cage everyone inside. Room-wide, so it scales with the crowd.
mechanics.brief: Spend essence on a room to blast, drain, and wither everyone inside; late acts cage them too.

mechanics.text: Soul Harvest — the Lich slowly regenerates while it holds essence; hoard for a deadlier final fight or spend now for kill pressure.

flavorText: It does not raise the dead — it drinks them. Every soul your halls collect makes the withering deeper.

name: Serpent Captain

headline.name: The Plague-Bearer
headline.summary: Its bite carries a living plague. Infected adventurers spread the sickness body-to-body through the whole raid; banked VIRULENCE (per infected kill) makes it leap to more victims and tick harder all run.
headline.brief: Its bite carries a plague that spreads hero-to-hero through the raid; banked Virulence makes it leap farther and tick harder.

mechanics.text: Plague Spit (day active) — arm, click a room: infect everyone with a heavy dose that then spreads on its own — to neighbors (Act II), farther + a feverish slow (Act III), and across rooms with bursting corpses (Act IV).
mechanics.brief: Infect a whole room with a heavy dose that then spreads on its own into neighboring rooms.

mechanics.text: Venom Stack — Lizardman minion attacks stack poison that lingers for the rest of the run (still in effect alongside the boss's plague).

flavorText: What the eye doesn't see, the fang still finds.

name: Orc Veteran

headline.name: Trophy Hunter
headline.summary: Every hero class your dungeon kills arms the Veteran with their weapon — his throne-fight arsenal grows over the run and sharpens with repeat kills.
headline.brief: Every hero class you kill arms him with their weapon; his arsenal grows all run and sharpens with repeat kills.

mechanics.text: Trophy Throw (day active) — arm, click a room, and hurl one claimed weapon per trophy type into it: Blade rends, Heavy roots, Arcane hexes, Hunter slows, Faith heals the boss. Empowered trophies hit harder; higher acts throw more of the arsenal (T4 = all of it).
mechanics.brief: Hurl claimed weapons into a room — blades rend, heavy roots, arcane hexes, hunter slows, faith heals the boss.

mechanics.text: Warband — orcs in the same room buff each other's ATK and DEF, and each loots permanent ATK per kill.
Mastery (Act III+) — his most-claimed trophy radiates a dungeon-wide boon (Blade→minion ATK, Heavy→minion DEF, Arcane→traps recharge, Hunter→minion range, Faith→boss regen).

flavorText: He wears the realm's champions as trophies. Every hero you feed him teaches the axe a new way to kill.

name: Vampire Sovereign

headline.name: The Blood Sovereign
headline.summary: Runs the dungeon as a blood economy: every wound feeds a banked BLOOD pool spent on Blood Rites and a growing charmed Court.
headline.brief: Runs the dungeon as a blood economy — every wound banks Blood, spent on Blood Rites and a growing charmed Court.

mechanics.text: BLOOD — a cut of all damage dealt to heroes (and each kill) banks BLOOD; it heals the Sovereign and powers his abilities.
mechanics.brief: A cut of all damage and every kill banks Blood, which heals the Sovereign and powers his abilities.

mechanics.text: Blood Rite (day) — drain %maxHP from everyone in a room; by act it charms the lowest survivor, leaves a Sanguine Pool, then mass-converts the dying.
The Court — charm heroes into roaming Thralls; T4 a slain thrall erupts and charms a neighbor.

flavorText: Hospitality is a weapon. So are most things, in the right hands.

name: Succubus Queen

headline.name: The Rapture
headline.summary: Banks ALLURE and spends it to mesmerize the party — Infatuated turn on their own, Enraptured freeze defenseless (and take extra damage), Lured walk into your traps.
headline.brief: Banks Allure to mesmerize the party — the Infatuated turn on their own, the Enraptured freeze, the Lured walk into traps.

mechanics.text: ALLURE — every heart she turns banks ALLURE; it scales how many heroes each mesmerize hits and how long it lasts.
mechanics.brief: Every heart she turns banks Allure, scaling how many heroes each mesmerize hits and how long it lasts.

mechanics.text: Kiss of Rapture (day) — beguile a whole room: infatuate all, enrapture the wounded, lure survivors, and (late) freeze the room in mass ecstasy.
Doppelgänger — the Queen hides among seductive mirror-images you must shatter to land a real hit.

flavorText: She does not need a key. Doors are for the unloved.

name: Slime King
tagline: Splits when struck, leaks little ones

headline.name: Mitosis
headline.summary: A self-multiplying ooze: it buds free gooplings and absorbs the dead, swelling its Mass — the more Mass, the bigger it grows and the larger the horde it splits into in the throne fight.
headline.brief: A self-multiplying ooze — it buds free gooplings and absorbs the dead, swelling its Mass into a bigger throne-fight horde.

mechanics.text: Mitosis Surge (day active) — arm, click a room: flood it with a wave of gooplings that swarm everyone inside (scales with Mass + the crowd).
mechanics.brief: Flood a room with a wave of gooplings that swarm everyone inside, scaling with Mass and the crowd.

mechanics.text: The Horde — roaming gooplings coalesce into bigger slimes and leave acid trails; in the throne fight blobs split, recombine if not bursted, and (late acts) self-heal.

flavorText: There is no killing it. There is only making more of it.
